old_value: {"id":"broadside-kv75.1","title":"HIGH: DNS rebinding bypasses SSRF host check","description":"File: server.rs, Lines: 1133-1160. is_private_host() only checks hostname strings and IP literals, not DNS-resolved addresses. An attacker domain like evil.com pointing to 127.0.0.1 or 169.254.169.254 bypasses all SSRF guards. Affects: inbox Follow handler (actor fetch), actor_cache (key fetch), media (remote image download), delivery worker (inbox delivery). Fix: add async DNS resolution check before outbound requests. Resolve hostname, verify all resolved IPs against private ranges, fail closed on DNS failure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-18T17:08:27.847285208Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-18T17:08:27.847285208Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-kv75.1","depends_on_id":"broadside-kv75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-18T17:08:27.85487847Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Added is_private_host_resolved() with async DNS lookup. All 6 SSRF check points now resolve hostnames and verify IPs against private ranges. Fails closed on DNS failure.
